title: poa trivialis subsp. feratiana
intro: Poa trivialis subsp. feratiana, also known as Rough Meadow-grass, is a subspecies of grass native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It is used in traditional medicine for its expectorant and diuretic properties, which can help relieve respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and urinary tract problems, such as kidney stones. The plant is rich in flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, providing health benefits like reducing inflammation, improving immune function, and preventing infections. Herbal preparations of Poa trivialis subsp. feratiana include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety.
